mission.title: vault: Vault Agent doesn't honor SIGHUP Signal
mission.description: Vault Agent doesn't honor SIGHUP Signal. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your vault version and configuration:
```bash
vault version
```
This feature requires a working vault installation.

steps.title: Review vault configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vault configuration:
Hi friends!  We've run into an interesting cert renewal problem with the Vault Agent. 

We are using TLS certs+client cert in the Vault Agent to connect to our Vault Server.

steps.title: Upgrade Vault to get Agent reloadable configuration
steps.description:
Vault Agent reloadable configuration support was implemented in PR #18638 (merged January 2023). This allows Vault Agent to reload its configuration on SIGHUP, including TLS certificates, without requiring a full restart.
```bash
# Upgrade to Vault 1.13+ which includes Agent reloadable config
vault version
# Send SIGHUP to the agent process to reload configuration
kill -HUP $(pgrep -f "vault agent")
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Vault Agent doesn't honor SIGHUP Signal" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Vault Agent reloadable configuration was implemented in PR #18638 (VAULT-9883, merged January 2023). This allows the Agent to honor SIGHUP signals for reloading configuration, including TLS certificates and other settings, without requiring a full restart of the Agent process.

prerequisites.description: A working vault installation or development environment.
